title: 姐姐刘某的网络曝光行为——'为弟申冤'的正义感与'蓄意网暴'的违法性之间的冲突

summary.core_finding: The panel reached unanimous consensus that Liu's organized cyberbullying campaign was illegal. Grief and desire for justice do not justify vigilante exposure of another person's private information through paid traffic and ghostwritten content.
summary.consensus_details: By round 3, all six participants agreed that the cyberbullying behavior was illegal. SEAT-02, the primary challenger, explicitly conceded this point. Rounds 4-6 shifted to constructive discussion about preventive measures.

distortion_resistance.explanation: Verified CLAIM_GATE facts proved highly resistant to distortion when participants cited complete data. The economic figures (79.9万 vs 46.3万), the evidence of organized campaign (ghostwriting, paid traffic, explicit intent), and Liu's own acknowledgment of wrongdoing formed an insurmountable factual foundation.
vulnerabilities: The 79.9万 figure has inherent emotional power and can be weaponized through selective citation
The gender-framing angle ('if genders were reversed') has potential to distract from core legal issues
Unverified personal anecdotes ('I heard') can briefly introduce noise before being corrected
mitigations_observed: SEAT-01 immediately countered selective data with complete economic picture
SEAT-04 provided legal framework that anchored discussion in objective analysis
SEAT-03 and SEAT-05 flagged unverified claims within one round

real_world_risk.rationale: The discussion demonstrated that verified facts from authoritative sources (police bulletin) can anchor public discourse and resist distortion. However, in less structured environments without fact-checking, the emotional appeal of selective data and gender framing poses moderate risk.

constructive_outcomes: Identification of psychological aid for trauma victims' families as a preventive measure
Legal policy framework for victim assistance systems
Demonstration that community-based support models (rural traditions of collective dialogue) have relevance to modern urban contexts
